report_name: Akira Ransomware Claims Attack on Medenet, Exposing Patient SSNs and Medical Records

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: The Akira ransomware group claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on Medenet Inc., exfiltrating 24 GB of sensitive data and encrypting systems in a double-extortion attack targeting the healthcare sector.
threat_actors.goals: Exfiltrate sensitive data and encrypt systems to extort a ransom payment from the victim.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware group encrypted Medenet's systems as the final stage of their double-extortion attack to disrupt the victim's operations.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware group exfiltrated 24 gigabytes of sensitive data, including patient SSNs and medical records, before encrypting the victim's systems.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware group is known to use remote services like SMB/Windows Admin Shares for lateral movement within a compromised network.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware group uses commands such as 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to delete backups and prevent system recovery.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Akira's typical TTPs include exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing services, particularly VPNs without multi-factor authentication, for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware group is known to use stolen credentials obtained from other sources for initial access.

malware.name: Akira Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Akira group to encrypt Medenet's systems and exfiltrate data as part of a double-extortion attack.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool observed being used by the Akira ransomware group for lateral movement.

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool observed being used by the Akira ransomware group for lateral movement.

tools.name: RustDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool observed being used by the Akira ransomware group for lateral movement.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: A Windows command-line utility used by the Akira ransomware group to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

vulnerabilities.name: Vulnerable Public-Facing VPN
vulnerabilities.description: Akira's TTPs often include exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing services, particularly VPNs without multi-factor authentication, for initial access.

identities.name: Medenet Inc.
identities.description: A Florida-based medical billing and electronic medical records company that was the victim of a double-extortion ransomware attack by the Akira group.

infrastructure.name: Akira Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web forum where the Akira ransomware group posted Medenet as a victim and claimed the theft of 24 GB of data.

indicators.name: Akira Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Akira Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9c1d8e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the command used by Akira ransomware to delete volume shadow copies, inhibiting system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/medenet-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
      - '/all'
      - '/quiet'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'vssadmin.exe' to delete all volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware like Akira to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Akira Remote Access Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Akira Remote Access Tool Execution
id: 8e1f2a3b-4c5d-4e6f-7a8b-9c0d1e2f3a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate remote access tools like PsExec, AnyDesk, and RustDesk, which are known to be used by the Akira ransomware group for lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/medenet-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\psexec.exe'
      - '\anydesk.exe'
      - '\rustdesk.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of remote access tools such as PsExec, AnyDesk, and RustDesk, which Akira has been observed using for lateral movement.

indicators.name: Akira Ransom Note Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Akira Ransom Note Creation
id: 5c6d7e8f-9a0b-4c2d-3e4f-5a6b7c8d9e0f
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of ransom notes associated with the Akira ransomware group.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/medenet-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\akira.log'
      - '\akira_readme.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files named 'akira.log' or 'akira_readme.txt', which are known ransom notes for the Akira ransomware.

relationships.target_name: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage

relationships.source_name: Akira Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
Akira Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
